ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for canned green peas; SNOMED CT coverage for processed legume products is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of canned green peas is approximately 85 g (about half a cup drained), providing roughly 59 kcal, 3.8 g protein, and 3.5 g dietary fibre.

nutrient_source.title: Peas, green, canned, regular pack, drained solids — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 170419)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for canned green peas (drained solids) used as primary nutrient reference. Core macro and micronutrient values are consistent with the HPB Singapore input values (energy 69 kcal, protein 4.42 g, fat 0.35 g, carbohydrate 12.58 g, fibre 4.1 g, sodium 2 mg, calcium 20 mg, iron 0.95 mg). HPB values are retained as the primary record values given the Singapore HPB provenance stated in the input; USDA SR Legacy serves as corroborating approved source. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, cholesterol, potassium, vitamin C, and other micronutrients populated from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 170419.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 48 for canned green peas is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and corroborated by Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283, which lists canned peas at GI ~48 (glucose reference = 100). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving / 100 = 48 × (12.58 × 0.85) / 100 = 48 × 10.69 / 100 ≈ 5.1; rounded to 5.8 using net carbohydrate after fibre adjustment (12.58 − 4.1 = 8.48 g net carb per 100 g; per 85 g serving = 7.21 g net carb; GL = 48 × 7.21 / 100 ≈ 3.5). Using total carbohydrate per serving: 12.58 × 0.85 = 10.69 g; GL = 48 × 10.69 / 100 = 5.1. Reported as 5.1 rounded to 5.1; value set to 5.1 in record. Note: value in JSON field set to 5.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (2 mg/100 g) and meaningful dietary fibre (4.1 g/100 g) supporting vascular health
hypertension.guidance: Canned green peas with no added salt are an excellent choice for hypertensive individuals due to their negligible sodium content (2 mg/100 g); however, consumers must verify the product label, as some commercial brands add salt raising sodium to 200–300 mg/100 g, and rinsing canned peas under water can reduce sodium by up to 40%.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 48), moderate carbohydrate (12.6 g/100 g), high fibre (4.1 g/100 g), and plant protein (4.4 g/100 g) moderating postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Canned green peas are a low-GI legume (GI 48) with 4.1 g dietary fibre and 4.4 g protein per 100 g, which together slow gastric emptying and blunt postprandial glucose excursions, making them suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in standard portions (approximately 85 g per serving) and paired with no-added-salt varieties.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.06 g/100 g), zero trans fat, high dietary fibre (4.1 g/100 g), and plant-based protein profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Canned green peas are cardioprotective due to their negligible saturated fat (0.06 g/100 g), absence of trans fat, and 4.1 g/100 g dietary fibre which supports LDL cholesterol reduction; they are suitable as a regular component of a heart-healthy dietary pattern, provided low-sodium or no-added-salt varieties are selected.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory micronutrient profile including vitamin C (8 mg/100 g), iron (0.95 mg/100 g), and dietary fibre (4.1 g/100 g) with negligible fat and no trans fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Canned green peas provide vitamin C, iron, and dietary fibre that support anti-inflammatory pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; individuals with RA should monitor personal legume tolerance and select no-added-salt varieties to avoid sodium-driven tissue oedema.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Canned green peas are commercially processed garden peas (Pisum sativum) that have been shelled, blanched, and heat-sterilised in sealed cans with water or a light brine solution. The canning process preserves the peas' characteristic mild, sweet flavour and soft, slightly firm texture, making them a convenient pantry staple used globally in soups, stews, rice dishes, salads, and as a side vegetable. Per 100 g, canned green peas provide approximately 69 kcal, 4.4 g protein, 0.35 g fat, 12.6 g carbohydrate, 4.1 g dietary fibre, and only 2 mg sodium (in no-added-salt varieties). They are a low-glycemic-index food (GI 48) that is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. The high fibre content supports digestive health and LDL cholesterol reduction, while the plant protein contributes to satiety. Canned green peas are naturally vegan, gluten-free, halal, and kosher. They are widely available in Singapore supermarkets and are used across Western, South Asian, and Southeast Asian cuisines. Consumers should check product labels for added salt, as sodium content can vary significantly between brands.
common_user_queries: Are canned peas good for diabetics?
Canned peas vs fresh peas nutrition
Can hypertensive patients eat canned peas?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced from Singapore HPB (input provenance, confidence 0.95) and corroborated by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 170419) for canned green peas — an exact food match in an approved primary source. GI value of 48 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2008). All mandatory fields are complete and supported.
notes: 1. Sodium value of 2 mg/100 g reflects no-added-salt canned green peas as reported by HPB Singapore. Commercial brands with added salt may contain 200–300 mg sodium/100 g; consumers should verify product labels. 2. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 170419 — canned green peas contain no trans fat by product composition). cholesterol_mg is set to 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-based food contains no dietary cholesterol). vitamin_b12_mcg is set to 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients). vitamin_d_mcg is set to 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy). 3. Glycemic load per serving calculated using total carbohydrate: GL = GI × carbohydrate per serving / 100 = 48 × (12.58 × 0.85) / 100 = 48 × 10.69 / 100 ≈ 5.1. 4. No multilingual local-language names are required for this food as it is a generic Western/international processed food product, not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish requiring mandatory local-language coverage under G6. 5. FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code are defensible best-match entries; SNOMED CT code not identified for this specific processed legume product. 6. Potassium value of 96 mg/100 g sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 170419, consistent with the hypertension health context note in the input record.
